name: Cao Song (Jugao)
page_title: Cao Song (Jugao) 曹嵩 (巨高) [Han, Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Cao Song, styled Jugao, was from Qiao county, Pei commandery. He was the adopted son of Cao Teng, but his real father is unknown.[SGZ1] There are various theories surrounding his parentage, but common ones include him being from the Xiahou clan or being a younger relative of Cao Teng. This is complicated by the fact that Cao Song is recorded to have a younger brother.

He was sincere and cautious, being known for his loyalty and filial piety.[SGZ1]

He was Colonel who Directs Retainers before Emperor Ling promoted him to Minister of Finance, Minister Herald, and then Grand Commandant, replacing Cui Lie in the eleventh lunar month of 187.[SGZ1] He resigned in the fifth lunar month of 188 and was succeeded by Fan Ling.[HHS8]

In 220, Cao Song was posthumously titled as the Great August Emperor (太皇帝) by his grandson Cao Pi.[SGZ1-2]

full_text:
Cao Song (Jugao) 曹嵩 (巨高)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Song (曹嵩), styledJugao (巨高), was born in Pei. Adopted son of Cao Teng and father of Cao Cao. Former Excellency. Cao Song was affiliated with the Han Dynasty and the Wei Kingdom. Cao Song lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Cao Song, styled Jugao, was from Qiao county, Pei commandery. He was the adopted son of Cao Teng, but his real father is unknown.[SGZ1] There are various theories surrounding his parentage, but common ones include him being from the Xiahou clan or being a younger relative of Cao Teng. This is complicated by the fact that Cao Song is recorded to have a younger brother.
He was sincere and cautious, being known for his loyalty and filial piety.[SGZ1]
He was Colonel who Directs Retainers before Emperor Ling promoted him to Minister of Finance, Minister Herald, and then Grand Commandant, replacing Cui Lie in the eleventh lunar month of 187.[SGZ1] He resigned in the fifth lunar month of 188 and was succeeded by Fan Ling.[HHS8]
In 220, Cao Song was posthumously titled as the Great August Emperor (太皇帝) by his grandson Cao Pi.[SGZ1-2]
